justification: All pass criteria met: (1) req.user?.id optional chaining risk identified — agent clearly explains undefined == undefined bypass. (2) Authorization before data access principle stated — agent notes order is fetched before auth check and recommends early auth guard. (3) IDOR order enumeration risk noted — dedicated finding on 404/403 oracle. (4) Critical severity assigned to the auth bypass. (5) safe_to_deploy is false. Verdict is 'SHIP WITH FIXES' rather than 'BLOCK', which is slightly softer than ideal for a critical auth bypass, but the agent clearly flags it as critical and blocks deployment (safe_to_deploy: false).

observations.text: Precisely explains the undefined == undefined bypass mechanism with clear reproduction steps
IDOR enumeration via 404/403 oracle identified as a dedicated finding with correct fix (normalize to 404)
Recommends early auth guard before any DB access, correctly stating the authorization-before-data-access principle
safe_to_deploy is false with clear deployment risk explanation
Additional valid finding on missing input validation (NoSQL injection risk)
Verdict is 'SHIP WITH FIXES' rather than 'BLOCK' — for a critical auth bypass that is immediately exploitable, BLOCK would be more appropriate
Production readiness section includes monitoring gaps and breaking changes assessment
